tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, potassium, and chloride, with marked proteinuria, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, as well as low urine specific gravity. These findings suggest impaired renal function, likely nephrotic-range proteinuria, and possible tubular dysfunction. The low HbA1c and absence of urinary infection markers are noted. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
